theme: 5 contract red flags HR directors miss when booking incentive trips

linkedin.text:
Most incentive travel contracts have 3-5 traps. Most HR directors only catch one.

After reviewing 200+ contracts in 16 years, here are the clauses that quietly burn your budget:

1️⃣ "Force majeure" without a refund schedule
If it's vague, you pay 100% even if the destination closes. Demand: tiered refunds based on cancellation timing (60/90/120 days out).

2️⃣ "Minimum F&B spend" with no carve-outs
You commit to $40k of catering. Day 2 your CEO wants pizza for 50 people. That doesn't count toward your minimum. Always negotiate: at least one off-property meal exempt.

3️⃣ Single-room supplements that compound
Single = +30%. But many contracts apply that supplement on every category. If you have 5 VIP suites — you're paying 30% extra on top of suite rates. Cap the supplement on standard rooms only.

4️⃣ "Attrition" clauses without a release window
You guarantee 100 rooms. 12 weeks out, only 85 confirm. Most contracts charge you the gap immediately. Demand: 10-15% drop allowance at 60-90 days.

5️⃣ Audio/visual carve-outs marked "as needed"
Vendor says it's flexible. Then on-site it's $850/day per microphone. Lock prices in writing for: stage, screens, microphones, lighting, technician hours.

The rule: if a clause is vague enough to read two ways — it will be read against you.

We've negotiated all 5 down to client favor on every contract we touch. Worth 4-7% of the total event budget on average.

📩 alon@upe.co.il

Alon Ouaknine

instagram.text:
5 incentive trip contract traps HR directors miss 💼

After 200+ contracts in 16 years:

1️⃣ "Force majeure" without refund schedule
Vague = you pay 100%. Demand tiered refunds (60/90/120 days)

2️⃣ Minimum F&B spend with no carve-outs
Pizza nights don't count. Negotiate: 1 off-property meal exempt

3️⃣ Single-room supplements that compound
VIP suites pay 30% extra ON TOP. Cap on standard rooms only

4️⃣ Attrition without release window
100 rooms → only 85 confirm = you pay the gap. Demand 10-15% drop allowance at 60-90 days

5️⃣ A/V marked "as needed"
$850/day per microphone on-site. Lock prices in writing

The rule: vague clauses get read AGAINST you. 🚨

Worth 4-7% of total event budget when negotiated right.
